up.method: only O2/CH4 consumption ratio reported for this condition (1.73); absolute CH4 uptake not stated in main text for sMMO
O2/CH4 consumption ratio = 1.73 (measured); absolute O2 uptake not stated in main text

sec.method: HPLC of benzoylated putrescine; wild-type titer in absence of copper (excreted into medium)

mfa.note: experimentally measured under sMMO-expressing (copper-free) condition; GEM predicted 1.66

growth_records.cite: Yang, Junwon et al. A systems-level view of metabolic impact of copper and methane valorization potential in Methylosinus trichosporium OB3b. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2026). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2026.e00272. [PMC13091384]
growth_records.snippet: Under sMMO-expression conditions, the GEM predicted an 8 % higher growth rate (0.067 hr-1) and a 4 % lower O2/CH4 consumption ratio (1.66) compared to the measured values (0.062 and 1.73 hr-1, respectively)
growth_records.notes: Wild-type M. trichosporium OB3b, sMMO-expressing (0 uM copper), NMS, 30 C, methane:air 1:2. MEASURED growth rate mu=0.062 h-1 (GEM predicted 0.067). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.062 = 11.18 h. MEASURED O2/CH4 consumption ratio = 1.73 (GEM predicted 1.66); absolute per-biomass CH4 and O2 uptake not given in main text for this condition. Putrescine excreted by wild-type = 1.6 umol/gDCW in absence of copper (from 'putrescine in dgdh decreased from 1.6 to 0.7 umol/gDCW compared to the wild-type'). All values are experimental measurements from this study.

growth_records.medium: Nitrate mineral salt (NMS) medium + 10 uM copper (CuCl2)

up.method: only O2/CH4 consumption ratio reported (1.20); absolute CH4 uptake not stated in main text for pMMO growth-comparison
O2/CH4 consumption ratio = 1.20 (measured); absolute O2 uptake not stated

sec.method: HPLC; wild-type titer in presence of 10 uM copper (excreted into medium)

mfa.note: experimentally measured under pMMO-expressing (10 uM copper) condition; DC-mode GEM prediction differed by 12%

growth_records.cite: Yang, Junwon et al. A systems-level view of metabolic impact of copper and methane valorization potential in Methylosinus trichosporium OB3b. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2026). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2026.e00272. [PMC13091384]
growth_records.snippet: The predicted and measured growth rates, 0.106 and 0.104 hr-1 respectively, only differed by 2 % when using the RA or UeT modes ... the measured O2/CH4 consumption ratio ... an 12 % difference from the measured ratio of 1.20
growth_records.notes: Wild-type M. trichosporium OB3b, pMMO-expressing (10 uM copper), NMS, 30 C, methane:air 1:2. MEASURED growth rate mu=0.104 h-1 (GEM RA/UeT predicted 0.106). doubling_time = ln2/0.104 = 6.66 h. MEASURED O2/CH4 consumption ratio = 1.20. Copper accelerates growth (0.062 -> 0.104 h-1) vs copper-free sMMO condition. Putrescine excreted by wild-type = 1.6 umol/gDCW in presence of copper (from 'from 1.6 to 1.0 umol/gDCW in [presence]'). Absolute per-biomass CH4/O2 uptake not given for this growth comparison; see separate 13C-MFA record for measured CH4 uptake flux.

mfa.note: experimental measurement; steady-state 13C-MFA ESTIMATE was 12.4 mmol/gDCW/h (significantly lower than measurement)
model-estimated, not experimental; shown for contrast with 18.7 measured

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: EMC cycle flux (fraction of serine-cycle/SHMT flux)
mfa.note: 13C-MFA: 28% of flux entering serine cycle utilized by EMC (ethylmalonyl-CoA) cycle in wild-type; closely aligned with GEM

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: forward TCA entry (SHMT flux to alpha-ketoglutarate)
mfa.note: GEM prediction for wild-type; 13C-MFA instead indicated reversed alpha-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase / reversed aKG direction

growth_records.cite: Yang, Junwon et al. A systems-level view of metabolic impact of copper and methane valorization potential in Methylosinus trichosporium OB3b. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2026). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2026.e00272. [PMC13091384]
growth_records.snippet: The steady-state 13C-MFA estimated methane uptake flux was significantly lower than the measurement (12.4 vs 18.7 mmol/gDCW-hr; Fig. S7A) ... the measured growth rate of dargE::PmxaFargJ decreased from 0.092 hr-1 in the wild-type to 0.056
growth_records.notes: Wild-type M. trichosporium OB3b 13C-MFA condition (25% 13CH4/75% 12CH4, NMS, 30 C, methane:air 1:2). MEASURED specific methane uptake = 18.7 mmol/gDCW/h (13C-MFA model estimate 12.4). MEASURED wild-type growth rate mu=0.092 h-1 (stated as the wild-type value from which dargE::PmxaFargJ mutant fell to 0.056). doubling_time = ln2/0.092 = 7.53 h. Copper status of the 13C-MFA cultures not explicitly stated in main text. 18.7 mmol/gDCW/h is an experimental measurement; 12.4 is a model estimate (recorded in mfa_fluxes but flagged non-experimental).

up.method: 13C-MFA: methane-uptake gap between estimate and measurement 'alleviated' vs wild-type but still less estimated than measured; no absolute number in main text

sec.method: HPLC; dargE::PmxaFargJ with 10 uM copper - highest titer observed (0.7 umol/gDCW without copper)

mfa.note: 13C-MFA: up to 47% of methane assimilation (SHMT) flux drawn into forward TCA cycle in mutant (vs 5% GEM/reversed in wild-type)

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: EMC cycle flux (fraction of SHMT flux)
mfa.note: 13C-MFA: EMC cycle decreased from 28% (wild-type) to 15% in this mutant

growth_records.cite: Yang, Junwon et al. A systems-level view of metabolic impact of copper and methane valorization potential in Methylosinus trichosporium OB3b. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2026). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2026.e00272. [PMC13091384]
growth_records.snippet: the measured growth rate of dargE::PmxaFargJ decreased from 0.092 hr-1 in the wild-type to 0.056 ... 47 % of methane assimilation flux (SHMT) drawn into the forward TCA cycle ... EMC cycle significantly decreased from 28 % of SHMT flux in t
growth_records.notes: Engineered mutant dargE::PmxaFargJ, NMS, 30 C, methane:air 1:2, 13C-MFA. MEASURED growth rate mu=0.056 h-1 (vs 0.092 for wild-type). doubling_time = ln2/0.056 = 12.38 h. Putrescine excreted 2.8 umol/gDCW with 10 uM copper (highest observed), 0.7 umol/gDCW without copper. Absolute methane uptake flux not numerically stated for the mutant. 13C-MFA central-carbon splits captured.
